revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ageshima Kofun]][[ja:上島古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Ageshima Kofun | native_name =上島古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ageshima Kofun, Izumo.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ageshima Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ageshima Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.42333333333333|N|132.8|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|qid=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|24|N|132|48|00|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Ageshima Kofun'''|上島古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]] in the Kunitomi-chō neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|qid=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}}, {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}}, Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162058|title= 上島古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021|qq=}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middleof the 6th century, towards the end of the Kofun period. ==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. Many Kofuns are located at the eastern foot of Mt. Tabushi, including the Ageshima Kofun. The location overlooks {{ill|Lake Shinji|en|Lake Shinji|qid=Q1151248|lt=Lake Shinji|1=Lake Shinji}}. This tumulus was discovered in 1949 during land clearance. It is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}} style Kofun with a diameter of 15 meters and a height of two meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface of the mound. There are two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one containing a house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} and the other a pit-style stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The house-shaped sarcophagus is carved out of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}, measuring 1.83 meters in length and 0.69 meters in width. In addition to human remains, a large quantity of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} was discovered. These included a "five-bell" {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, a silver ring, 184 beads (10 agate beads, 22 glass round beads, 152 small glass beads), six gilt-bronze guard band decorations, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery and an iron sword. However, many burial goods that were confirmed to exist at the time of the excavation and photographed in situ were subsequently lost and their current whereabouts is unknown. The surviving artifacts are kept at the Ageshima Kofun Hosankai, and some are on display at the Shimane Prefectural Museum of Ancient Izumo History.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The pit stone chamber was constructed from a combination of natural stone and split stone, and is 1.85 meters long, 0.6 to 0.7 meters wide and 0.7 meters high. No human remains were found, and the only grave goods was two complete sets of horse harnesses and fittings, along with 47 arrow heads, a bow fitting and a large amount of Sue pottery pottery. <ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tabushi Station|en|Tabushi Station|qid=Q11504488|lt=Tabushi Station|1=Tabushi Station}} on the {{ill|Ichibata Electric Railway|en|Ichibata Electric Railway|qid=Q128426|lt=Ichibata Electric Railway|1=Ichibata Electric Railway}} {{ill|Kita-Matsue Line|en|Kita-Matsue Line|qid=Q11352976|lt=Kita-Matsue Line|1=Kita-Matsue Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/event/kodai/publication.data/kodai29_02.pdf Shimane Prefecture report]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21249 Shimane Prefectural Tourism Federation home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.city.izumo.shimane.jp/www/contents/1238718535840/files/P16.pdf Izumo City home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
